benchmark_details.name: R-Bench (Relationship Hallucination Benchmark)
benchmark_details.overview: R-Bench is a novel benchmark for evaluating relationship hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), featuring image-level and instance-level questions focused on the existence of relationships and assessing local visual comprehension.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To analyze and evaluate relationship hallucinations in Large Vision-Language Models.

data.source: Based on the nocaps validation set, which consists of images from OpenImage and curated manually to prevent overlap with pre-trained data of LVLMs.
data.annotation: Combination of automatic generation by Large Language Model and manual curation.

methodology.calculation: Metrics are computed based on evaluation of model responses to yes/no questions.
methodology.interpretation: Higher scores reflect better model understanding of visual relationships and lower hallucination rates.
methodology.validation: Question evaluation through visual validation to ensure logical consistency.
